standard_description: Lines are taken to lines, and line segments to line segments of the same length.
subject: Math

problems.text:
Triangle
$${{{CDE}}}$$
underwent a transformation that created triangle
$${{{C'D'E'}}}$$
.
###IMAGE0###
a.   Describe how triangle
$${{{CDE}}}$$
was transformed to become triangle
$${{{C'D'E'}}}$$
.
b.   What features stayed the same?
c.   What features changed?
d.   Is triangle
$${{{CDE}}}$$
congruent to triangle
$${{{C'D'E'}}}$$
? Explain how you know.

The two triangles in the picture below are congruent:
###IMAGE0###
a.   Give a sequence of rotations, translations, and/or reflections that take
$${\triangle PRQ}$$
to
$${\triangle ABC}$$
.
b.   Is it possible to show the congruence in part (a) using only translations and rotations? Explain.

Problem 1
Triangle
$${{LMN}}$$
underwent a single transformation to become triangle
$${{PQR}}$$
, shown below.
###IMAGE0###
a.   What single transformation maps triangle
$${{LMN}}$$
to triangle
$${{PQR}}$$
? Describe in detail.
b.   Name two things that are the same about both triangles.
c.   Name two things that are different about the triangles.
Problem 2
Perform the following rotations on figure
$${{ABCD}}$$
below.
###IMAGE1###
a.    Rotate figure
$${{ABCD}}$$
$${90^{\circ}}$$
counter-clockwise around point
$$A$$
to create
$$A'B'C'D'$$
.
b.   Rotate figure
$${{ABCD}}$$
$${180^{\circ}}$$
counter-clockwise around point
$$A$$
to create
$$A''B''C''D''$$
.

Problem 1
Triangle
$${ABC}$$
is translated 3 units down and 5 units to the right to create triangle
$${A’B’C’}$$
.
###IMAGE0###
Which statement is true?
Problem 2
Angle
$${ABC}$$
is translated as shown below. Use the translation to answer the questions that follow.
###IMAGE1###
a.   Describe the translation and name the new angle.
b.   Will
$${\overleftrightarrow{AB}}$$
and
$${\overleftrightarrow{A'B'}}$$
ever intersect? Explain your reasoning.
c.   If the measure of
$$\angle {ABC}$$
is
$${70^{\circ}}$$
, what is the measure of
$${\angle A'B'C'}$$
? Explain your reasoning.
Problem 3
Below are the coordinate points from an original figure and a translated figure.
$$A(1,2) \rightarrow A' (-1, -1)$$
$$B(-1,4) \rightarrow B'(-3,1)$$
$$C(5,4) \rightarrow C' (3,1)$$
Describe the translation that occurred.

Problem 1
You want to prove that
$${{ABCD}}$$
and
$${{A'B'C'D'}}$$
are congruent by using a translation. Explain how you could translate
$${{ABCD}}$$
onto
$${{A'B'C'D'}}$$
so that they overlap perfectly. Be specific and use the coordinate plane as a reference.
###IMAGE0###
Problem 2
Figure
$${ABC}$$
is shown in the coordinate plane.
###IMAGE1###
a.    Translate figure
$${ABC}$$
3 units to the right and 2 units up. Name and label the new figure.
b.   How are the two figures the same?
c.   How are the two figures different?
Problem 3
Figure 1 is congruent to Figure 2.
###IMAGE2###
Which statement demonstrates the congruency between the two figures?

Problem 1
Triangle
$${{ABC}}$$
has been moved according to the following sequence: a translation followed by a rotation followed by a reflection.
With precision, describe each rigid motion that would map triangle
$${{ABC}}$$
onto triangle
$${LMN}$$
.
###IMAGE0###
Problem 2
Avik believes that Figure 1 and Figure 2, shown below, are congruent because he can use two reflections to map one figure to the other.
Sonya also believes the two figures are congruent because she can use a single rotation to map one figure to the other.
###IMAGE1###
Are both Avik and Sonya right? Explain with precision the transformation(s) that will show the two figures are congruent.
Problem 3
Rectangle
$${{ABCD}}$$
and
$${{A'B'C'D'}}$$
are shown below.
###IMAGE2###
Which sequence of transformations show that rectangle
$${{ABCD}}$$
and rectangle
$${{A'B'C'D'}}$$
are congruent? Select all that apply.

Problem 1
Watch the 5 videos of Ms. Pac-Man from from Robert Kaplinsky's
How Did They Make Ms. Pac-Man?
. After each video, discuss the question in each part.
a.   Watch the first video of Ms. Pac-Man under “The Situation.”
Discuss: How can you describe Ms. Pac-Man’s movements?
b.   Watch the next video (“Translations Only”).
Discuss: What do you think “translation” means? What other movements did Ms. Pac-Man make in the original video?
c.   Watch the next video (“Translations and Reflections Only”).
Discuss: What do you think “reflection” means? Did Ms. Pac-Man make any other movements in the original video?
d.   Watch the next video (“Translation, Reflections, and Rotations”).
Discuss: How can we get more precise to describe how far she translates, in what direction she rotates, etc.?
e.   Lastly, watch the video with the coordinate plane (“Translations, Reflections, Rotations, and Coordinate Plane”).
Discuss: How does the coordinate plane help us communicate about movements more precisely?
Problem 2
For each pair of figures, decide whether these figures are the same size and same shape. Be prepared to justify your reasoning. You may use mathematical tools to make your decision.
###TABLE0###
Problem 3
For each set, determine if the two figures are congruent. Explain how you would move one figure to get to the other figure, and what transformations you would use.
a.
###IMAGE0###
b.
###IMAGE1###
c.   Lines
$$P$$
and
$$Q$$
are parallel and are transformed to map onto lines
$$P'$$
and
$$Q'$$
###IMAGE2###

Problem 1
Triangle
$${{ABC}}$$
is rotated
$${90^{\circ}}$$
clockwise around the origin.
###IMAGE0###
Which of the statements below are true? Select all that apply.
Problem 2
Describe how you can use transformations to show that the two figures below are congruent.
###IMAGE1###

Figure 1 is shown in the coordinate plane below. Four other figures are shown: Figures
$${A, B, C,}$$
and
$$D$$
.
###IMAGE0###
a.   Which of the four figures are congruent to Figure 1?
b.   For two of those figures identified in part (a), describe a sequence that proves the congruency between Figure 1 and the other figure.

Figure
$${QRSTU}$$
undergoes a translation that takes it 4 units left and 2 units down. Use the diagram to answer the questions that follow.
###IMAGE0###
a.   What are the coordinates of point
$${T'}$$
?
b.   What are the coordinates of point
$${Q'}$$
?
c.   Name two line segments in the translated figure that will be parallel to
$${\overline{UT}}$$
.
d.   Name three features that will be the same in the original figure and the translated figure.
e.   Name one feature that will be different in the original figure and the translated figure.

Two figures are shown in the coordinate plane. Alex thinks that the two figures are congruent because figure
$${QRS}$$
could be translated 5 units to the left and 2 units down to map to figure
$${Q'R'S'}$$
.
###IMAGE0###
a.   Explain why Alex’s thinking is incorrect.
b.   How would you change figure
$${Q'R'S'}$$
so it is congruent to figure
$${QRS}$$
? What translation would prove the congruence between the two figures?

Below is a picture of a triangle on a coordinate grid:
###IMAGE0###
a.   Draw the reflection of
$${\triangle ABC}$$
over the vertical line through
$${x=-2}$$
. Label the image of
$$A$$
as
$$A'$$
, the image of
$$B$$
as
$$B'$$
, and the image of
$$C$$
as
$$C'$$
.
b.   Draw the reflection of
$${\triangle A'B'C'}$$
over the vertical line through
$$x=2$$
. Label the image of
$$A'$$
as
$$A''$$
, the image of
$$B'$$
as
$$B''$$
, and the image of
$$C'$$
as
$$C''$$
.
c.   What single rigid transformation of the plane will map
$${\triangle ABC}$$
to
$${\triangle A''B''C''}$$
? Explain.

Problem 1
Figure 1 is reflected over the
$$y$$
-axis to create Figure 2, as shown in the coordinate plane below.
###IMAGE0###
a.   Describe what you notice about the coordinate points of a figure when it is reflected over the
$$y$$
-axis.
b.   What do you think happens to coordinate points of a figure when it is reflected over the
$$x$$
-axis?
Problem 2
Triangles
$${{ABC}}$$
and
$${{PQR}}$$
are shown below in the coordinate plane.
Describe a sequence of transformations that can map
$${{ABC}}$$
to
$${{PQR}}$$
.
###IMAGE1###

Problem 1
Figure 1 is shown in the coordinate plane below.
###IMAGE0###
Which figure(s) would Figure 1 map to if it were
a.   Translated?
b.   Reflected?
c.   Rotated?
Problem 2
Below is a picture of two rectangles with the same length and width:
###IMAGE1###
a.   Show that the rectangles are congruent by finding a translation followed by a rotation that maps one of the rectangles to the other.
b.   Explain why the congruence of the two rectangles cannot be shown by translating Rectangle 1 to Rectangle 2.
Problem 3
Figures
$${J, K, L, M, N,}$$
and
$$P$$
are shown on the coordinate plane.
###IMAGE2###
a.   Which figure can be transformed into Figure
$$P$$
by a translation 2 units to the right followed by a reflection across the
$$x$$
-axis?
###TABLE0###
b.   Which figure can be transformed into Figure
$$L$$
by a 90-degree rotation clockwise about the origin followed by a translation 2 units down?
###TABLE1###

In each diagram below, determine if Figure 2 can be obtained by a single reflection of Figure 1. If yes, then describe the reflection.
a.
###IMAGE0###
b.
###IMAGE1###
c.
###IMAGE2###

Triangle
$${QRS}$$
was transformed to create triangle
$${Q'R'S'}$$
in the coordinate plane below.
###IMAGE0###
a.   Describe the transformation that maps
$${QRS}$$
to
$${Q'R'S'}$$
.
b.   Rotate figure
$${Q'R'S'}$$
$${90^{\circ}}$$
clockwise around the origin and draw the new figure
$${{Q''R''S''}}$$
. Describe a single transformation that maps triangle
$${QRS}$$
to
$${{Q''R''S''}}$$
.

Problem 1
Figure
$${ABCDEF}$$
is shown in the coordinate plane below. Trace the figure on a piece of patty paper, then use it to investigate the questions that follow.
###IMAGE0###
a.   Use your patty paper to reflect the figure so that point
$$F$$
maps to point
$$X$$
. Draw the reflected image in the coordinate plane. What do you observe about the reflection?
b.   Use your patty paper to reflect the original figure so that point
$$F$$
maps to point
$$Y$$
. Draw the reflected image in the coordinate plane. What do you observe about the reflection?
c.   What impact does reflecting an image have on its orientation? How is this different from a translation?
Problem 2
Triangle
$${EFG}$$
is reflected across a line of reflection to map to triangle
$${E'F'G'}$$
as shown below. What is the line of reflection?
###IMAGE1###
